engaged_works.summary: This on-disk text is being wired into the Source tab as a citation witness for Judith Butler, Butler Gender Trouble 202409. The entry is intentionally minimal: it records the routed file and basic bibliographic shape so the site can surface the source while fuller review waits. A later pass should tighten the title, edition, and passage coverage against the on-disk witness.

core_thesis: Butler argues that gender is not the expression of a prior essence but the effect of repeated norms that produce the appearance of natural identity ([Gender Trouble ch. 3](cite://butler/gender-trouble/3); [Bodies That Matter, Introduction](cite://butler/bodies-that-matter/introduction)). The subject does not stand outside these norms, since the very terms of intelligibility are socially instituted. Agency therefore lies in the unstable reiteration of the norm, where citation can misfire and be resignified.
